text:
There once was an old oak tree in the center of the most lively forest circle. It hadn't moved in centuries, its sturdy branches entangled with those of countless other trees. The animals around it knew it well; it was their history textbook.

Every dawn, the sun would poke its fiery fingers through the leaves, turning the forest's canopy a riotous array of colors. The squirrels taught young birds the nuts' secret codes. The owls called timeless Histories from their perches.

Our tale begins on a particular evening. A young fox, Elio, sat perched on a gnarled branch, staring thoughtfully at the old oak. Elio hadn't gotten a serious education, but he'd absorbed wisdom from all sorts of elders in his village. Tonight, he felt an itch to learn something more profound.

"I've noticed," thought Elio to himself, "that the way stars fade like memories, guides us into wisdom." He pondered this, and remembered an
